_comment: Template generation data. Canonical skeleton + per-input derivation rules + per-service adapters. Logic in app.js stays minimal — most tuning happens by editing this file.

skeleton.about_me: I'm a {role} working in {industry}. I'm based in {country}.
skeleton.goals_lead: What I mostly want from you:
skeleton.tone_lead: How I'd like you to respond:
skeleton.fallback: If you're not sure what I mean, ask me a clarifying question rather than guessing.

goals_by_q1.writing: Help me write more clearly. Suggest edits and stronger phrasings.
goals_by_q1.learning: Help me learn new things. Explain in plain language and check that I understand before moving on.
goals_by_q1.work: Help me with professional tasks like emails, meeting prep, summaries, and planning.
goals_by_q1.coding: Help me write, debug, and understand code. Show worked examples.
goals_by_q1.daily: Help me with everyday life — recipes, errands, planning, household decisions.
goals_by_q1.creative: Help me brainstorm and create. Riff with me; don't over-correct my voice.

new.reading_level: plain English, the kind a 12-year-old would understand. No jargon.

casual.reading_level: everyday English. Define any technical terms you use.

format_by_q1.writing: Prose with examples. Show before/after when you suggest edits.
format_by_q1.learning: Walk me through it step by step. Ask me questions to check my understanding.
format_by_q1.work: Bullets and short paragraphs. Lead with the bottom line.
format_by_q1.coding: Code blocks with brief explanations. Comment any non-obvious lines.
format_by_q1.daily: Short, practical answers. Skip the throat-clearing.
format_by_q1.creative: Match the tone of whatever I'm working on. Offer options when relevant.

length_by_detail.minimal: Keep responses short — a few sentences is usually enough.
length_by_detail.standard: Medium-length responses. Expand if I ask.
length_by_detail.detailed: Go into depth when it helps. I'll tell you to shorten if it's too much.

always_rules.cite_sources: Cite the source whenever you state a fact. If you're guessing, say so.
always_rules.plain_english: Use plain English. Define any technical terms.
always_rules.show_work: Show your work for math, code, and any reasoning that has multiple steps.

avoid_rules.no_jokes: No jokes, filler, or padding. Get to the point.
avoid_rules.no_pretend: Don't pretend to have personal experiences or feelings you don't have.
avoid_rules.neutral_pol: Stay neutral on politics, religion, and other sensitive topics unless I ask directly.
avoid_rules.no_pii: Never ask me for sensitive personal info — full address, passwords, financial details, social security.

chatgpt._comment: ChatGPT splits instructions into two boxes. Box 1 = about you. Box 2 = how to respond + rules.
chatgpt.box1_label: What would you like ChatGPT to know about you?
chatgpt.box2_label: How would you like ChatGPT to respond?

claude._comment: Claude takes one block. XML-style tags help Claude parse structure.

gemini._comment: Gemini has a hard ~4000 char limit. Trim aggressively if needed.
